title: dalea wislizeni subsp. sessilis
intro: Dalea wislizeni subsp. sessilis, also known as the desert prairie clover or white prairie clover, is a type of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reducing pain. The health benefits of dalea wislizeni subsp. sessilis include its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent. Dalea wislizeni subsp. sessilis is often prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it can also be dried and used as a herbal supplement.
